questions.question: Which therapeutic approach focuses on identifying and modifying distorted thinking patterns that contribute to emotional distress?
questions.explanation: Cognitive therapy, pioneered by Aaron Beck, focuses on identifying and changing distorted thinking patterns (such as catastrophizing and all-or-nothing thinking) that maintain emotional distress. It helps clients develop more balanced, realistic perspectives.

questions.question: Systematic desensitization is a behavioral therapy technique used primarily to treat:
questions.explanation: Systematic desensitization, developed by Joseph Wolpe, reduces anxiety through the gradual pairing of relaxation with increasingly anxiety-provoking stimuli. It is widely used to treat phobias and other anxiety-related conditions.
